question.text: Under SEBI's mutual fund categorization, an Ultra Short Duration Fund invests in debt and money market instruments with a Macaulay duration of __________.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI categorizes debt mutual funds by Macaulay duration. The Ultra Short Duration Fund has a Macaulay duration of 3 to 6 months. The complete duration spectrum is: Overnight (1 day), Liquid (up to 91 days), Ultra Short (3-6 months), Low Duration (6-12 months), Short Duration (1-3 years), Medium Duration (3-4 years), Medium to Long Duration (4-7 years), and Long Duration (more than 7 years).
**The Trap:** 1-3 months overlaps with Liquid fund territory (up to 91 days). 6-12 months is the Low Duration Fund category. 1-2 years falls within the Short Duration Fund range. Students must memorize the exact duration boundaries for each category.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Debt fund duration categories are tested in NISM Series XII and V-A (Mutual Fund). The entire duration spectrum from Overnight to Long Duration is a high-frequency test topic.
**Key Takeaway:** Ultra Short Duration = 3 to 6 months Macaulay duration. Remember the sequence: Liquid (up to 91 days) -> Ultra Short (3-6 months) -> Low Duration (6-12 months) -> Short (1-3 years).
